context.projectOverview: Ports the Claude Agent SDK agent-loop capabilities into agent-bober's own provider-agnostic loop as additive, default-off changes. Sprints 1-4 implement the four architecture-backed high-value gaps (refusal detection, cost substrate + USD budget ceiling, per-role effort, parallel read-only tools); sprints 5-10 implement the remaining capability areas (loop events + hooks, sessions/resume/fork, in-context compaction, streaming deltas, mid-turn interrupt, in-process subagents + opt-in MCP bridge) in the own-loop idiom per explicit user direction, bound by the same hard constraints.

contract.title: Refusal detection end-to-end (stopReason 'refusal' + fail-closed generator)
contract.description: Surface a model refusal as a first-class outcome instead of a silent success. Add an explicit 'refusal' StopReason mapping in the Anthropic and OpenAI-family adapters, detect it at the loop's completion branch, return refused:true on AgenticLoopResult (never throw), and make parseGeneratorResult fail closed: a refusal overrides the filesWritten-implies-success path. Advisory/read-only roles surface the refusal text without failing the run (ADR-5).

successCriteria.description: The Anthropic adapter's normalizeStopReason maps the raw 'refusal' stop reason to StopReason 'refusal' via an explicit case (not the default branch), and the open StopReason union documents 'refusal' as a known value.
The OpenAI-family adapter maps its refusal surface (refusal content / content_filter finish reason) to StopReason 'refusal'.
runAgenticLoop returns { refused: true, stopReason: 'refusal' } with a resolved (not rejected) promise when the client returns a refusal, including when tool results from earlier turns exist.
parseGeneratorResult returns success:false with a refusal note when loopResult.refused === true, even when filesWritten.size > 0 (the refusal guard runs before the filesWritten check).
When no refusal occurs, AgenticLoopResult carries no refused key and all pre-existing loop/generator tests pass unchanged (byte-identical non-refusal path).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass with the extended types.

nonGoals: Do not add budget, effort, or parallel-tool behavior (later sprints).
Do not throw from the loop on refusal (ADR-5: return, never throw — no catcher exists around runGenerator in pipeline.ts).
Do not change advisory roles' success semantics — they surface refusal text as output.
Do not touch the claude-code adapter's text-only boundary.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: adapter refusal mapping (both families), loop refused flag without throwing, generator fail-closed override of filesWritten, and byte-identical non-refusal behavior.
contract.definitionOfDone: A provider refusal propagates as StopReason 'refusal' through the adapters, sets refused:true and stopReason 'refusal' on AgenticLoopResult without throwing, and a generator sprint that refuses after partial writes is reported success:false. Non-refusal runs are byte-identical. Build, typecheck, lint, and the full suite are green.
assumptions: The Anthropic SDK's raw StopReason already includes 'refusal' (node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk .../messages.d.ts) and currently passes through normalizeStopReason's default branch as a raw string; the substantive bug is the loop's completion branch treating any non-tool_use stop as done. Evidence: src/providers/anthropic.ts:32-45, src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts:301.
StopReason (src/providers/types.ts:207) is an open union ('end' | 'tool_use' | 'max_tokens' | 'error' | string) so adding 'refusal' is additive.
No code outside providers/ and agentic-loop.ts reads AgenticLoopResult.stopReason (verified at architecture Checkpoint 4), so the new value cannot break existing consumers.
contract.generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) src/providers/anthropic.ts normalizeStopReason (~:32-45): add an explicit case mapping 'refusal' → 'refusal' with a one-line comment that the value is part of the open union. (2) src/providers/openai.ts normalizeStopReason (~:164-175): map the OpenAI refusal surface (message.refusal content and/or finish_reason 'content_filter') → 'refusal'; openai-compat shares this path. (3) src/providers/types.ts: document 'refusal' in the StopReason union comment; add optional refused?: boolean is NOT needed on ChatResponse — the loop derives it. (4) src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts completion branch (~:301, the stopReason !== 'tool_use' return): when response.stopReason === 'refusal', return the normal result shape plus refused: true (spread-conditional so the key is absent otherwise). (5) src/orchestrator/generator-agent.ts parseGeneratorResult (~:181-260): accept the loop result (it already receives it or thread it), and FIRST check refused === true → return success:false with a clear note ('model refused: <finalText excerpt>') before any filesWritten logic. Follow existing test file collocation (*.test.ts next to source) and use fake LLMClient objects returning scripted ChatResponses like existing agentic-loop tests do.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Use a fake client whose chat() returns stopReason 'refusal' (with and without prior tool_use turns) and assert the loop resolves with refused:true, stopReason 'refusal' (sc-1-3). Unit-test normalizeStopReason in both adapters directly (sc-1-1, sc-1-2). For sc-1-4 call parseGeneratorResult with a loop result where refused=true and a non-empty filesWritten set; assert success:false and the note mentions refusal. For sc-1-5 assert a normal completion result has no 'refused' key (use Object.hasOwn) and run the FULL existing suite. Confirm build + typecheck + lint all pass.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
